domains.name: marshall
domains.description: Marshall Fire mass tort — Boulder County, Colorado, December 30, 2021. Representing homeowners, renters, and business owners affected by the most destructive wildfire in Colorado history.

nouns.name: IntakeSubmission
nouns.description: A public submission from a potential Marshall Fire claimant seeking a free case review.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Unique identifier.

attributes.name: phone
attributes.description: Best contact phone number.

attributes.name: address
attributes.description: Street address of the affected property.

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: One of the three affected jurisdictions.

attributes.name: damage_type
attributes.description: Primary category of loss or harm.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Free-form description of what happened and what was lost.

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Internal review state.

attributes.name: submitted_at
attributes.description: Timestamp the intake was submitted.

verbs.name: Submit
verbs.description: A public visitor submits the intake form.

verbs.name: Qualify
verbs.description: Intake staff qualify the submission as a legitimate claimant.

examples.description: Total loss of primary residence and two vehicles on Dec 30, 2021.
Smoke and heat damage to contents; roof and siding partially destroyed.

nouns.name: Claimant
nouns.description: A person whose intake submission has been reviewed and qualified as a legitimate Marshall Fire victim pursuing a legal claim.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Unique identifier.

attributes.name: date_of_birth
attributes.description: Used for identity verification.

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Lifecycle state of the claimant.

attributes.name: intake_submission_id
attributes.description: Reference to the originating IntakeSubmission.

attributes.name: assigned_attorney_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Attorney assigned to represent this claimant.

attributes.name: registered_at
attributes.description: When the claimant record was created.

attributes.name: assigned_at
attributes.description: When an attorney was assigned.

verbs.name: Register
verbs.description: Creates a claimant record from a qualified intake submission.

verbs.name: View
verbs.description: Retrieve details for a specific claimant.

verbs.name: Assign
verbs.description: Assign an attorney to represent this claimant.

nouns.name: Claim
nouns.description: A specific cause of action filed by a Claimant against the defendants responsible for the Marshall Fire. A single Claimant may file multiple Claims (e.g., real property and personal injury).

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Unique identifier.

attributes.name: claimant_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Claimant filing this claim.

attributes.name: damage_type
attributes.description: Category of loss or harm being claimed.

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Litigation state of the claim.

attributes.name: estimated_value
attributes.description: Estimated monetary value of the claim in USD.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Narrative description of the loss and damages.

attributes.name: filed_at
attributes.description: When the claim was formally filed.

attributes.name: updated_at
attributes.description: When the claim status was last changed.

verbs.name: File
verbs.description: Formally submit a claim on behalf of a claimant.

verbs.name: Review
verbs.description: Legal staff review the claim details and supporting evidence.

verbs.name: UpdateStatus
verbs.description: Advance or change the status of a claim (e.g., to settled or dismissed).

examples.description: Total loss of primary residence at 1284 Paragon Dr, Superior CO. Structure destroyed; lot remains.
Smoke and heat damage to personal property including furnishings and electronics.

nouns.name: Incident
nouns.description: The Marshall Fire event itself — a singleton record anchoring all claims to the specific wildfire that occurred in Boulder County, Colorado on December 30, 2021.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Unique identifier.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Official name of the disaster event.

attributes.name: date
attributes.description: Date the incident occurred.

attributes.name: jurisdiction
attributes.description: Legal jurisdictions affected (county, municipalities).

attributes.name: affected_areas
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of affected communities (Superior, Louisville, Unincorporated Boulder County).

attributes.name: homes_destroyed
attributes.description: Approximate number of homes destroyed (reported: ~1,084).

attributes.name: homes_damaged
attributes.description: Approximate number of homes damaged (reported: ~149).

attributes.name: statute_of_limitations_date
attributes.description: Colorado tort filing deadline for this incident. Governs whether new claims can be accepted.

nouns.name: Property
nouns.description: A real-property address within the Marshall Fire affected zone. Linked to one or more Claims; used to verify jurisdiction and assess property damage.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Unique identifier.

attributes.name: state
attributes.description: Always CO for Marshall Fire.

attributes.name: parcel_number
attributes.description: County assessor parcel number for official identification.

attributes.name: damage_assessment
attributes.description: Official or estimated damage level for this parcel.

attributes.name: claimant_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Claimant who owns or occupied this property.

attributes.name: in_affected_zone
attributes.description: Whether this address falls within the verified Marshall Fire damage perimeter.

verbs.name: Assess
verbs.description: Record or update the official damage assessment for this property.

nouns.name: Evidence
nouns.description: A supporting document uploaded by a claimant or staff member to substantiate a Claim — such as insurance policies, photographs, damage assessments, receipts, or government records.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Unique identifier.

attributes.name: claim_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Claim this evidence supports.

attributes.name: document_type
attributes.description: Category of the document.

attributes.name: file_name
attributes.description: Original file name of the uploaded document.

attributes.name: file_url
attributes.description: Secure storage URL for the uploaded file.

attributes.name: file_size_bytes
attributes.description: File size in bytes.

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Verification state of this evidence item.

attributes.name: uploaded_by
attributes.description: ID of the user (claimant or staff) who uploaded this evidence.

attributes.name: uploaded_at
attributes.description: When the document was uploaded.

attributes.name: verified_at
attributes.description: When the document was verified by staff.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Staff notes from the verification review.

verbs.name: Upload
verbs.description: A claimant or attorney uploads a supporting document to a claim.

verbs.name: Verify
verbs.description: Staff confirm the document is authentic and relevant to the claim.

nouns.name: CaseStatus
nouns.description: A milestone record tracking the progression of a Claimant's overall case through the mass-tort pipeline — from intake through settlement or dismissal.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Unique identifier.

attributes.name: claimant_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Claimant this case status tracks.

attributes.name: stage
attributes.description: Current milestone stage of the case.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Staff notes explaining the current stage or last transition.

attributes.name: advanced_by
attributes.description: ID of the staff member or attorney who last advanced the case stage.

attributes.name: advanced_at
attributes.description: Timestamp of the last stage advancement.

verbs.name: Advance
verbs.description: Move a case to the next milestone stage.

examples.notes: Complaint filed in Boulder County District Court.

nouns.name: Firm
nouns.description: A participating law firm in the Marshall Fire mass-tort coordination network. Supports multi-firm coordination where multiple firms jointly represent claimants.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Unique identifier.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Official firm name.

attributes.name: bar_number
attributes.description: State bar registration number.

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Participation status of the firm in the mass tort.

attributes.name: onboarded_at
attributes.description: When the firm was approved and activated in the system.

verbs.name: Onboard
verbs.description: Approve and activate a law firm to participate in the mass-tort matter.

nouns.name: Attorney
nouns.description: An individual attorney at a participating Firm who is assigned to represent one or more Claimants in the Marshall Fire mass-tort matter.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Unique identifier.

attributes.name: bar_number
attributes.description: Individual state bar number.

attributes.name: firm_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Firm this attorney belongs to.

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Whether this attorney is currently accepting assignments.

verbs.name: Assign
verbs.description: Assign this attorney to represent a Claimant.

capabilities.name: IntakeSubmission.Submit
capabilities.description: Records a new intake submission from a public visitor.

capabilities.name: IntakeSubmission.Qualify
capabilities.description: Intake staff review a received submission and qualify it, which triggers creation of a Claimant record.

capabilities.name: Claimant.Register
capabilities.description: Creates a Claimant record from a qualified IntakeSubmission.

capabilities.name: Claimant.View
capabilities.description: Retrieve full profile and case details for a specific Claimant.

capabilities.name: Claimant.Assign
capabilities.description: Assign an Attorney to represent a Claimant.

capabilities.name: Claim.File
capabilities.description: Formally file a cause of action for a Claimant.

capabilities.name: Claim.Review
capabilities.description: Legal staff review the claim details and supporting evidence.

capabilities.name: Claim.UpdateStatus
capabilities.description: Advance or change the status of a claim (e.g., to settled or dismissed).

capabilities.name: Evidence.Upload
capabilities.description: Upload a supporting document to a claim.

capabilities.name: Evidence.Verify
capabilities.description: Staff confirm a document is authentic and relevant.

capabilities.name: CaseStatus.Advance
capabilities.description: Move a case to the next milestone stage.

capabilities.name: Firm.Onboard
capabilities.description: Approve and activate a law firm to participate in the mass-tort matter.

capabilities.name: Attorney.Assign
capabilities.description: Assign an attorney to represent a Claimant.

capabilities.name: Claimant.Withdraw
capabilities.description: A claimant voluntarily exits the case. All open claims are dismissed and the claimant record is marked withdrawn.

capabilities.name: Property.Assess
capabilities.description: Record or update the official damage assessment for a property, sourced from county records, insurance reports, or on-site inspection.

causes.description: Public visitor submits the intake form from the marketing site.
Intake staff member reviews a received submission and marks it as qualified.
Automatically triggered when an IntakeSubmission is qualified, creating the Claimant record.
Attorney or staff member retrieves claimant details.
Intake staff assigns an attorney to a newly registered claimant.
Attorney formally files a claim on behalf of a claimant.
Legal staff initiate a review of a filed claim.
Attorney or staff updates the status of a claim following a legal development.
Claimant or attorney uploads a supporting document to a claim.
Staff member reviews and verifies an uploaded document.
Attorney or staff advances the case to the next milestone stage.
System administrator onboards a new participating law firm.
Triggered by the Claimant.Registered signal to prompt attorney assignment workflow.
Claimant or case manager initiates voluntary withdrawal from the case.
Paralegal records damage assessment from county records, insurance report, or site visit.

rules.description: Intake is open to the public. No authentication required.
Property must fall within the affected Boulder County jurisdictions.
Only intake staff or admins may qualify a submission.
A submission must be in received or under_review state before it can be qualified.
Only intake staff or admins may register a claimant.
Attorneys and intake staff may view claimant profiles.
Only intake staff or admins may assign an attorney to a claimant.
Claimant must be in registered or active state to receive an attorney assignment.
Attorneys and intake staff may file claims on behalf of claimants.
A claim may only be filed for a claimant who has been assigned an attorney.
Attorneys and intake staff may review claims.
Only filed claims are eligible for review.
Only attorneys or admins may update the status of a claim.
Claimants (owning the claim) and legal staff may upload evidence.
Evidence may not be uploaded to settled or dismissed claims.
Only legal staff or attorneys may verify evidence.
Attorneys and intake staff may advance a case stage.
Only system admins may onboard a new firm.
Only intake staff or admins may assign an attorney.
Only active attorneys may be assigned to claimants.
Only the assigned attorney or an admin may process a withdrawal.
Only claimants who have not already settled or withdrawn may withdraw.
Paralegals and admins may record property damage assessments.

outcomes.description: Records the submission and starts the review workflow.
Marks the submission as qualified and initiates claimant registration.
Creates the Claimant record and sets status to registered.
Records the attorney assignment and advances the claimant to assigned status.
Formally files the claim and sets its status to filed.
Advances the claim to under_review status.
Updates the claim status to the new value.
Creates an evidence record with status pending.
Records the stage advancement and timestamp.
Activates the firm and records the onboarding timestamp.
Links the attorney to the claimant.
Marks the claimant as withdrawn. Open claims should be dismissed as a downstream effect.
Records the damage assessment level for the property.

positions.name: IntakeSpecialist
positions.description: Reviews public intake submissions and qualifies or rejects them.

positions.name: Paralegal
positions.description: Manages evidence, files claims, and handles case status updates.

positions.name: CaseManager
positions.description: Oversees the intake-to-resolution process and manages staff assignments.

positions.name: PartnerAttorney
positions.description: Senior attorney overseeing the Marshall Fire mass-tort case.

positions.name: AssociateAttorney
positions.description: Attorney assigned to represent individual claimants.

positions.name: FirmAdmin
positions.description: Manages firm entities and attorney roster on the platform.

tasks.name: qualify-intake
tasks.description: Intake specialist reviews a public submission and qualifies or rejects it.

tasks.name: assign-claimant
tasks.description: Case manager assigns a qualified claimant to an attorney.

tasks.name: withdraw-claimant
tasks.description: Case manager processes a claimant's voluntary withdrawal from the case.

tasks.name: assess-property
tasks.description: Paralegal records the official damage assessment for a claimant's property.

tasks.name: file-claim
tasks.description: Paralegal formally files a claim on behalf of a claimant.

tasks.name: review-claim
tasks.description: Attorney reviews the filed claim for legal sufficiency.

tasks.name: update-claim-status
tasks.description: Attorney or paralegal updates the claim disposition (settled or dismissed).

tasks.name: upload-evidence
tasks.description: Paralegal uploads supporting evidence for a claim.

tasks.name: verify-evidence
tasks.description: Case manager verifies uploaded evidence for authenticity and relevance.

tasks.name: advance-case-status
tasks.description: Paralegal advances the case through milestones.

tasks.name: onboard-firm
tasks.description: Admin registers a new firm on the platform.

tasks.name: assign-attorney
tasks.description: Admin assigns an attorney to a firm.

processes.name: ClaimantIntake
processes.description: Public intake submission through qualification and attorney assignment. Claimant.Register fires automatically via signal after qualification; the staff workflow is qualify → assign.

steps.description: Intake specialist reviews the submission and decides qualification.
Case manager assigns qualified claimant to an attorney.

processes.name: CasePreparation
processes.description: After attorney assignment, three workstreams run in parallel: property damage assessment, evidence collection + verification, and case milestone advancement. All three must complete before the attorney review.

steps.description: Paralegal records damage assessment from county records or insurance report.
Paralegal uploads supporting documentation (photos, receipts, insurance correspondence).
Case manager verifies uploaded evidence for authenticity.
Paralegal advances case milestone to 'filed' stage.
Attorney reviews the complete case package — property assessment, verified evidence, and case status all converge here.

processes.name: ClaimResolution
processes.description: Takes a prepared claim through filing, legal review, and resolution. Branching: if the attorney approves, the claim proceeds to settlement or dismissal; if not, it returns for additional preparation.

steps.description: Paralegal formally files the claim with the court.
Attorney reviews the filed claim for legal sufficiency.
Claim disposition updated to settled or dismissed based on negotiation outcome.
Case milestone advanced to settled or dismissed.

processes.name: EvidenceCollection
processes.description: Standalone evidence workflow that can run repeatedly per claim. Each piece of evidence goes through upload then verification before it's admissible.

steps.description: Case manager reviews the document for authenticity and relevance.

processes.name: ClientWithdrawal
processes.description: Process for handling a claimant's voluntary withdrawal from the case. The case manager reviews the request, processes the withdrawal (which marks the claimant as withdrawn), and advances the case status to dismissed.

steps.description: Case manager processes the claimant's voluntary withdrawal.
Open claims are updated to dismissed status.

signals.name: marshall.IntakeSubmission.Received
signals.description: Emitted when a public intake form is submitted. Triggers the internal review queue.

signals.name: marshall.Claimant.Qualified
signals.description: Emitted when an intake submission is qualified by staff. Triggers claimant registration.

signals.name: marshall.Claimant.Registered
signals.description: Emitted when a Claimant record is created. Triggers attorney assignment workflow.

payload.name: claimant_id
payload.description: ID of the newly registered claimant.

signals.name: marshall.Claim.Filed
signals.description: Emitted when a Claim is formally filed. Triggers downstream court-filing integration.

equations.name: days_since_incident
equations.description: Computes the number of calendar days elapsed since the Marshall Fire (December 30, 2021). Used for statute-of-limitations checks — Colorado's two-year limit means new claims cannot be accepted once this value exceeds the incident's statute_of_limitations_date.

equations.name: estimated_claim_value
equations.description: Produces a rough estimated claim value in USD based on damage type and, where available, property damage assessment. Real property total-loss claims are valued higher than personal property or evacuation claims. This is a preliminary bucket estimate — not a legal settlement figure.

relationships.name: Claimant.intake_submission
relationships.description: Each Claimant originates from exactly one IntakeSubmission.

relationships.name: Claimant.attorney
relationships.description: Each Claimant may be assigned one Attorney; an attorney may represent many claimants.

relationships.name: Claim.claimant
relationships.description: Each Claim belongs to one Claimant; a Claimant may have multiple Claims.

relationships.name: Evidence.claim
relationships.description: Each Evidence document supports one Claim; a Claim may have many Evidence items.

relationships.name: Attorney.firm
relationships.description: Each Attorney belongs to one Firm; a Firm may have many Attorneys.

relationships.name: CaseStatus.claimant
relationships.description: Each CaseStatus milestone record belongs to one Claimant.

relationships.name: Property.claimant
relationships.description: A Property is associated with the Claimant who owns or occupied it.
